pr.title: Implement style check script and CI
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Tooling-only PR that added a Python 3.12 style-check module and CI workflow for two swept decomp style issues: raw JOBJ hidden flag literals in HSD_JObj flag calls and direct __assert calls that can use HSD assert macros. Review drove module layout, opt-out check flags, melee-issues-like output and exit codes, GitHub Actions annotations, tests kept out of CI, and src-only CI linting.

key_files.role: New CI workflow running tools/check/main.py with GitHub Actions annotations on push and pull_request paths including src, tools/check, and the workflow itself.
New style-check command-line implementation, registry, scanners, output modes, exit codes, and check definitions for jobj-flags and assert-macros.
Package marker and module documentation for tools/check.
Unit fixtures for assert-macros scanner cases: own-file __assert detection, foreign filename non-match, @todo exemption, macro-definition ignore, and split calls.
Unit fixtures for jobj-flags scanner cases: raw value-16 literal forms, line breaks, line/column reporting, comment/string handling, and non-matches.
Empty package marker shown in diff for check tests.

what_changed: Added tools/check/main.py as an extensible source style checker with a Check registry and opt-out flags generated as --no-<check>.

Added jobj-flags check for HSD_JObjSetFlags, HSD_JObjClearFlags, HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll, and HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll calls whose second argument is a raw integer literal value 16, recommending JOBJ_HIDDEN.

Added assert-macros check for direct __assert calls whose filename argument is __FILE__ or the current file basename, recommending HSD_ASSERT, HSD_ASSERTMSG, or HSD_ASSERTREPORT unless a nearby @todo marks no known byte-matching macro form.

Implemented shared scanning helpers that blank comments and string/char literals while preserving offsets, walk parentheses, find top-level commas, compute line/column, and ignore preprocessor directive lines for assert scanning.
Added std and actions output modes: std groups like melee-issues by severity, category, option, and file; actions emits GitHub Actions warning annotations.
Copied melee-issues-style exit behavior in the tool: 0 for clean, 65 for findings, and 74 for internal error.
Added unit fixtures for both checks under tools/check/tests, while CI runs only the source lint, not the unit tests.
Added .github/workflows/lint.yml to run python3 tools/check/main.py --msg-style actions src on push and pull_request when relevant paths change.
smart_moves: Kept the PR tooling-only after related source sweeps were split into separate PRs, reducing review scope and avoiding mixing lint infrastructure with mass source edits.
Made checks enabled by default with opt-out --no-<check> flags, matching reviewer guidance and making new style regressions visible unless explicitly disabled.
Used comment/string blanking plus parenthesis walking instead of simple line regexes, allowing detection of calls split across lines while avoiding comments and literals.
Added focused fixtures for edge cases reviewers called out, including line breaks, multiple literal forms, directive ignores, @todo exemptions, and non-matches.
CI uses --msg-style actions for inline annotations while retaining std output for local use.

decomp_lessons.lesson: After a source sweep removes a decomp style issue, add a narrow regression checker so the old generated/raw form does not silently return.

For checkers that reason about source calls, handle comments, string literals, nested parentheses, and split lines before reporting findings; simple token sets or single-line regexes are too fragile for reviewable CI linting.
When a direct __assert cannot yet be expressed with a byte-matching HSD assert macro, preserve that uncertainty with a nearby @todo rather than forcing a non-matching cleanup.

naming_conventions.convention: Check IDs are kebab-case and drive both CLI opt-out flags and output option labels, e.g. jobj-flags and assert-macros become --no-jobj-flags and --no-assert-macros.
The raw JObj hidden flag value should be referred to as JOBJ_HIDDEN in source-facing diagnostics.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:jobj-flags raw 0x10 where JOBJ_HIDDEN is meant

naming_conventions.convention: Direct __assert cleanup diagnostics should name the existing HSD assert macro family rather than prescribing a single macro in all cases.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:assert-macros direct __assert calls expressible with the HSD assert macros

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Treat @todo-annotated direct __assert sites as exempt when no byte-matching macro form is known, preserving matching uncertainty instead of forcing a cleanup.
For JObj flag cleanup, this checker intentionally catches standalone raw value-16 literals only and does not flag composite masks such as 0x10 | JOBJ_PTCL.
Future generalization was explicitly left as a TODO: parse arbitrary flag sets and resolve literals to defines rather than hard-coding only the hidden flag.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:TODO(future) generalize to resolving arbitrary raw literals

review_feedback.feedback: Rename and restructure the script, add opt-out CLI flags per check, use --msg-style actions, type annotate for Python 3.12, handle line breaks, use precompiled regex, and move self-tests to a separate local implementation.
Move from a single check.py/test_check.py layout to a tools/check module with check/main.py, __init__.py, and per-check tests under check/tests.
The CI should lint src on pull requests but should not run the unit tests, which are for local development of the tool.
std output should mirror melee-issues and copy its exit codes.
Incorporate the assert-macros check from the related PR before merging.
For the workflow, add src/** path filtering and adjust visible workflow/job/step names to Check code style, Check source for common style issues, and Run checks.
A future local CLI option to apply fixes would be useful, but was not required for this PR.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs adding auto-fix support to tools/check.
Find PRs extending jobj-flags from hard-coded JOBJ_HIDDEN to arbitrary JObj flag literal resolution.
Find failures or follow-up fixes for tools/check/main.py line/column reporting or comment/string stripping.
Find PRs adding more checks to CHECKS in tools/check/main.py.
Find source sweeps PR #2675 and PR #2676 for the code changes this tooling was meant to preserve.
Search for @todo annotations near __assert that exempt assert-macros.

notes: Evidence is strong for tooling behavior because the full diff and review comments are loaded.
No build, unittest, or CI execution logs are included beyond PR text and code; do not claim tests or CI passed except that the PR metadata says merged.
Changed-file metadata lists five files, while the raw diff also shows an empty tools/check/tests/__init__.py; intake records it as diff-supported but notes the metadata mismatch.

Review comments initially referenced paths tools/jobj_flags_check.py and .github/workflows/style-check.yml, while the final diff uses tools/check/main.py and .github/workflows/lint.yml; final paths are taken from the diff and changed-file metadata.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Turn completed decomp style sweeps into narrow regression checks
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2670 added tools/check and CI after related source sweeps, using targeted checks for raw JOBJ_HIDDEN literals and direct __assert macro opportunities so swept patterns fail future source lint.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep checker self-tests local while CI lints source
accepted_candidate_records.text: Reviewer clarified that tools/check tests are development fixtures and should not run in CI; CI should lint src on pull requests with actions-format annotations.

source_update_candidates.title: JOBJ_HIDDEN raw literal regression is enforced by tools/check
source_update_candidates.text: Consider noting that source should use JOBJ_HIDDEN, not standalone raw value-16 literals, in HSD_JObjSet/ClearFlags[All] calls; PR #2670 added a CI checker for this exact pattern.
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded standards include naming and macro cleanup principles but do not explicitly mention the tools/check enforcement point for JOBJ_HIDDEN.

source_update_candidates.title: Direct __assert cleanup can be linted, with @todo exemption for no known byte match
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding that direct __assert calls with __FILE__ or the current basename are linted as candidates for HSD_ASSERT/HSD_ASSERTMSG/HSD_ASSERTREPORT, while a nearby @todo may document a site where no byte-matching macro form is known.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is consistent with the existing assert-report-macros standard, but the @todo exemption and lint behavior are PR-specific evidence that may be useful to curate.

source_update_candidates.title: Potential future tools/check auto-fix option
source_update_candidates.text: Reviewer suggested a local CLI option to apply fixes instead of relying on an LLM to interpret findings; author planned to fold it into later tooling improvements.
source_update_candidates.reason: Follow-up idea was explicitly non-blocking and not implemented in this PR.

rejection_notes: Do not record that the checker catches all raw JObj flag literals; PR code only matches standalone value-16 literals for HSD_JObj Set/Clear Flags calls and explicitly leaves arbitrary flag-set resolution as future work.
Do not record that all direct __assert calls are forbidden; the checker exempts debug.h, preprocessor directives, foreign filename arguments, and nearby @todo sites.
Do not treat source sweeps #2675 and #2676 as changed in this PR; PR body says they were merged separately and this PR is tooling only.
Do not claim unit tests or CI passed from this slice; no execution logs are loaded.
